briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. The climate briefing for May 2, 2026, will now begin. Today's briefing will cover domestic and international efforts to address climate change, the escalating crisis, and the importance of transitioning to renewable energy.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Actions and Policy Trends
sections.content: Domestically, various stakeholders continue their efforts to address climate change. Jeonbuk Province, in collaboration with the Climate Change Research Institute, conducted a plogging event to promote the hosting of eco-friendly Olympics [1, 8, 9], while the Climate Change Center signed an MOU with Kangwon National University and Kookmin University for carbon talent cooperation [2], and proposed a transition to 'garden cities,' highlighting gardens and green spaces as key climate response infrastructure [3, 5]. Furthermore, Gyeonggi Province is accelerating the innovation of aging industrial complexes and carbon neutrality policies [13], and Asan City's festival demonstrated carbon neutrality through the use of reusable containers [14], showcasing concrete actions at the community level. Despite the continued government-level efforts, including the existence of the Presidential Committee on Carbon Neutrality and Green Growth [4, 6, 7, 15], a warning has been issued that while local governments' carbon neutrality plans have increased over the past five years, their implementation has been slow [16], indicating an urgent need to accelerate policy execution.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Economic Opportunities
sections.content: Renewable energy is now recognized not merely as an eco-friendly option but as a national security asset, with its importance being increasingly emphasized [17]. With 5,149 GW of renewable energy expected to be achieved by 2025 [18], solar and wind power are acting as key drivers in accelerating this transition. Investment experts are also recommending renewable energy as a weekly investment choice, highly valuing the growth potential of the related market [19], and positive economic outcomes are emerging in the renewable energy sector, such as HD Hyundai Energy Solutions recording a high operating profit margin of 18% due to solar power policy benefits [21]. At the regional level, pledges to transform Saemangeum into a hub for AI and renewable energy startups [20] further indicate that renewable energy is firmly establishing itself as a future growth engine.

sections.title: ⚠️ Serious Warnings from Global Climate Change
sections.content: Across the globe, severe crisis situations due to climate change are being reported. In the UK, questions have been raised about the disappearance of 'April showers' [22], Syria is facing a water scarcity crisis [25], and analyses from places like Phoenix, Denver, and Las Vegas indicate that the impacts of climate change are outpacing water conservation efforts [26], highlighting the severity of water insecurity. Harmful chemicals and climate change are jointly damaging the reproductive capabilities of various species [24], and news that an iconic bird is facing extinction due to minor climate change impacts on its habitat [31] signals a broader ecological crisis. The coral bleaching phenomenon in Mauritius, turning reefs ghost-white [33], and the observation that the climate crisis is fundamentally altering global poverty eradication efforts [32] warn of the destructive impacts of climate change extending beyond ecosystems to socio-economic systems as a whole. The argument to avoid excessive logging projects [28] also gains importance in this context.

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Response Discussions and Research
sections.content: The international community is actively pursuing various discussions and research to address climate change. In the shipping industry, a carbon tax has emerged as a 'hot issue' and is expected to be finally adopted within the year [10], signaling industry-wide changes. In New York, the Democratic Party is urging the courts to enforce climate change laws [29], increasing pressure for policy implementation. Former Vice President Al Gore is raising public awareness by delivering lectures on the climate crisis and its solutions in Nashville [30]. Meanwhile, academic and cultural approaches are also active, with a seminar being held on whether physics can stop climate change [23], and a permanent exhibition focusing on climate change opening at OMSI [27]. An interesting research finding reports that the South Asian region is bucking the global warming trend, with temperatures rising in the Himalayas while other areas are cooling [34]. News that a church in Massachusetts achieved carbon neutrality through various energy strategies [35] presents a practical case of carbon reduction, inspiring the international community.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has clearly illustrated the ongoing domestic and international efforts to combat climate change, alongside the deepening crisis. We must navigate this complex challenge through sustained policy implementation, technological innovation, and international cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing.
